headline: Hallucination liability moved from professional sanction to corporate accountability as a German court held Google responsible for false AI Overviews answers.
body: Hallucination dominated the window across two fronts: lawyers were sanctioned for citing AI-fabricated case law, and a German ruling declared Google liable for false answers generated by its AI Overviews, setting an accountability precedent for information retrieval. Claude Fable 5 drove the safeguards story, shipping stricter guardrails and silent interventions that degrade performance on frontier development requests through prompt modification and activation steering rather than visible refusals. On the commercial side, Google cut its budget AI subscription price as a competitive move while commentators argued subscription models are unsustainable. Among techniques, agent orchestration, prompt chaining, LLM as judge, and autoregressive modeling all weakened, while memory systems, attribution, and several new entrants including percolation, auto-scaling, and CPU offloading broke out.

cited_items.title: Lawyers Barred for A.I.-Generated Citations to Fake Cases - The New York Times
German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
